title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
While traveling on the "Twentieth Century Limited," Ellery Queen explains the details of a fourth murder to Professor Yadley, Inspector Quin, Vorn, and Escham. He describes a crime scene at a shabby house where a corpse was found nailed to a cross-shaped form. The victim had been dead for approximately fourteen hours. Outside the house, which featured a single door and a window sealed with barbed wire, Ellery observed two rows of footprints made by the same pair of shoes. These tracks, left after 11:00 PM following heavy rain, indicated a single person had entered and exited, and their pattern suggested the killer was lame.

Ellery identifies two critical pieces of evidence found on the stone floor: a used bandage roll and a large, unlabeled, opaque blue glass bottle of iodine. He notes that because the victim had no wounds on his wrists, the killer must have injured his own wrist, possibly while using an axe or struggling with the victim. Ellery points out a logical inconsistency regarding the medicine on the shelf: while there were two labeled bottles of disinfectant (iodine and red medicine), the killer specifically chose the unlabeled, non-transparent bottle. 

Ellery deduces that the killer must have had prior knowledge of the bottle's contents, implying the killer was the owner of the house, Andrew Tewell (who used the aliases Andrew Van and Old Pete). Consequently, Ellery concludes that Andrew Van is the killer and that the body nailed to the wall is actually a victim Van killed. To further the deception, Van dressed the victim in his own ragged clothes to make it appear as though Van himself were the deceased.
